before: int process(User* u, Order* o){ if(u!=nullptr){ if(u->isActive()){ if(o!=nullptr){ if(o->itemCount()>0){ return o->itemCount(); } else { return 0; } } else { return 0; } } else { return 0; } } return 0; }
after: int process(User* user, Order* order){ if (user == nullptr) return 0; if (!user->isActive()) return 0; if (order == nullptr) return 0; if (order->itemCount() <= 0) return 0; return order->itemCount(); }
why_safe: Same null checks and return values; nesting reduced with guard clauses only.

before: std::string classify(int score){ if(score>=90){ return "A"; } else { if(score>=80){ return "B"; } else { if(score>=70){ return "C"; } else { return "F"; } } } }
after: std::string classify(int score){ if (score >= 90) return "A"; if (score >= 80) return "B"; if (score >= 70) return "C"; return "F"; }
why_safe: All conditions and return values preserved; else-if chain flattened with early returns.
